comments.author: @mariadespina80
comments.text:
MOLECULAR PALEOGENETIC STUDY OF ANCIENT POPULATIONS FROM THE BRONZE AND IRON AGE IN ROMANIA. 2012.
Romanians are the ancient Dacians, 3000 years ago.
The Dacians did not disappear. We, Romanians, are their descendants, proven by paleogenetics.
"The continuity of the Daco-Romanians on this territory for 6000-7000 years, proven by the paleogenetic study from 2012-2016. We are the only autochthonous people in Europe." Here we were born... on this territory.
In 2012, a paleogenetic study was conducted in Germany by Mr. Prof. Dr. Alexander Rodewald, director of the Institute of Human Biology and Anthropology of the University of Hamburg, and Ms. Dr. Georgeta Cardoș, scientific researcher biologist, geneticist specialist throughout Romania, researching bones from prehistory and antiquity.   The Francisc Rainer Institute of Anthropology, of the Romanian Academy, provided most of the bone material studied. Another quantity of bone material was made available for the study by Mrs. Dr. Alexandra Comsa, from the former Institute of Thracology of the Romanian Academy. In total, the study had bone material available from over 20 sites in Romania (the Carpathian-Danubian-Pontic basin), from a number of 50 individuals belonging to ancient populations (22 from the Bronze Age, 28 from the Iron Age).
As for the blood samples from the current population of Romania, these were obtained at the Marius Nasta Institute and the Clinic of Phthisiology in Bucharest.
According to the conclusions of this study, the current population of Romania is clearly linked to the populations that lived on the territory of Romania in the Bronze and Iron Ages, that is, 2,500 – 5,000 years ago, which highlights the continuity of these peoples, despite all the vicissitudes of history. (Neolithic civilizations on Romanian territory: Cucuteni and Gumelnița, 7,500-8,000 years, Hamangia 6,000 years, Starcevo-Criș - 9,000 years, Turdaș-Vinca 7,500 years, Gârla Mare - 3,600 years, etc.)

Therefore, the study can tell us, among other things, who our true ancestors are. And our true ancestors are not the Romans, but the Thracian-Geto-Dacians. - Between the current population of Romania and the populations that lived on the territory of this country 2,500-5,000 years ago, there is a clear genetic relationship, which speaks of an undeniable continuity of the Romanian people on these lands. Even if there are also genetic traces belonging to the various migratory populations that passed through here, the basic genetic pool proves the continuity and connection with the ancient populations;

comments.author: @mariadespina80
comments.text:
Romania is the ancient kingdom of Dacia, 2000 years ago. Partially conquered by Emperor Trajan in 106 AD. Becoming a Roman province. In 1866 the name Romania was adopted from Rome-the capital of the Empire and the name  -român - (Romanian )from Romanus=citizen of Rome. Just as our ancestors the Dacians became in Dacia Felix-the Roman province.
  The Dacians, the ancestors of the Romanians, can be seen sculpted on Trajan's Column in Rome, erected in 113 AD by Emperor Trajan after conquering part of the Kingdom of Dacia .   The famous sculptor Apollodorus of Damascus created it .
   In Rome, there are over 100 statues of Dacians, all over 2.5 meters high. * Statues of Dacian commanders are found on the arch of Emperor Constantine the Great in Rome, erected in 315 AD. The statues are 3 meters high. And in the Vatican Museum there is a bust of King Decebal. All the Dacians appear very elegantly dressed and very beautiful people. And Decebalus.
